titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.8

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=656 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:34:59.979321+00 | translation moa=657 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:34:58.083145+00

div.p:
Alex’ . Dei gratia . Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue Salutem . Sciant presentes et futuri
nos pro salute anime nostre . et antecessorum et successorum nostrorum . dedisse . concessisse . et hac Carta
nostra confirmasse deo et beate Marie de Melros . et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus et in perpe
tuum seruituris . Lacum de Dunschor’ in valle de Nyth’ . et illam denariatam terre que pertinet
ad eundem Lacum . et quicquid continetur infra eundem Lacum et terram . Tenend’ . et habend’ .
eisdem Monachis in perpetuum . in Liberam . puram . et perpetuam elemosinam de nobis et heredibus nostris .
libere . quiete . plenarie . et honorifice . ab omni seruicio . consuetudine . et exaccione seculari . Nos
uero et heredes nostri omnia predicta eisdem Monachis contra omnes homines warantizabimus . nichil
iuris in eisdem  nobis retinentes . nec pro ipsis aliquid petentes preter orationes tantum . Ita tamen quod idem
Monachi singulis annis Die Sancti Andree de prouentibus eorundem habeant pitanciam . Testibus . G . electo .
Dunkeldensi . Laurentio . de Abbirnith’ . Bernardo fraser’ . Thoma filio . Ranulfi . Willelmo de Lyndeseia . Dauid
de Lyndes’ . Alexandro de striuelin’ . apud Alnecrumb tercio die Decembris . Anno Regni domini Regis
vicesimo Secundo .

Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land [sends] greeting. Let present and future know us, for the welfare of our soul, and of our predecessors' and successors' [souls], to have given, conceded, and by this our charter to have made firm, to God, St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that same place now and for ever in the future, the loch of Dunscore in Nithsdale, and that pennyland which belongs to the same loch, and whatever is contained within the same loch and land; for the same monks to hold and to have for ever, in free, pure, and perpetual alms, of us and our heirs, freely, quitly, fully, and honourably from all service, custom, and secular exaction. We and our heirs shall warrant all the aforesaid to the same monks against all men, withholding nothing of right in the same, nor requesting in return for them anything other than prayers only, provided that the same monks each year on St Andrew's day have a pittance from the profits of the same. As witnesses, Geoffrey, elect of Dunkeld; Laurence of Abernethy; Bernard Fraser; Thomas son of Ranulf; William of Lindsay; David of Lindsay; Alexander of Stirling. At Ancrum, on the third day of December in the twenty-second year of the reign of the lord king.
